commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a code repair that ClawSweeper can safely automate.; Cleared: The diff only changes Discord plugin startup ordering and a focused test, with no dependency, workflow, credential, permission, or supply-chain surface changes. (sha=8deff209b4f468f2a9e704adfc63ec18de367444)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reorders Discord startup delay slots so the configured `channels.discord.defaultAccount` starts before secondary accounts are staggered.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +46. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sorts Discord account ids through `listAccountIds`, so a configured `defaultAccount` such as `main` can receive a later startup delay when secondary account ids sort before it. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a code repair that ClawSweeper can safely automate.

target.title: Fix Discord default account startup priority
target.body:
Fixes #77429

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Discord multi-account startup delay ordering previously followed the configured account id list directly, so a configured primary account such as `channels.discord.defaultAccount: "main"` could be assigned a delayed startup slot when secondary account ids sorted before it.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw` on macOS, branch `codex/77429-discord-default-startup-fix`, using the production Discord channel gateway startup path in `extensions/discord/src/channel.ts` with the existing Discord channel test harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --write extensions/discord/src/channel.ts extensions/discord/src/channel.test.ts
$ pnpm test:extension discord extensions/discord/src/channel.test.ts
$ env OPENCLAW_TSGO_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
$ git diff --check -- extensions/discord/src/channel.ts extensions/discord/src/channel.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
pnpm test:extension discord extensions/discord/src/channel.test.ts
Test Files  160 passed (160)
Tests       1743 passed (1743)

env OPENCLAW_TSGO_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
exit code 0

git diff --check -- extensions/discord/src/channel.ts extensions/discord/src/channel.test.ts
exit code 0
```

Observed result after fix: the regression test configures `defaultAccount: "main"` with account ids ordered as `billy`, `farber`, then `main`. Starting `main` does not call `sleepWithAbort`, while starting `billy` calls the 10s delay and starting `farber` calls the 20s delay. This proves the configured primary account starts immediately even when the previous account ordering would have delayed it, and secondary accounts remain staggered.

What was not tested: no live Discord gateway with real bot tokens was started in this checkout; the proof uses the production Discord gateway startup function with mocked provider/probe network calls to isolate startup delay behavior.

## Supplemental validation

- Promotes the resolved Discord default account to the first startup slot only when it is enabled and configured for runtime startup.
- Preserves existing secondary account stagger behavior after the promoted default account.
- Does not change Discord usage UI, memory-core, Mattermost, or MCP code.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
